title: Your Song
why_this_song: Conversational self-deprecating narrative with lots of modal verbs — 'I hope', 'I wish', 'you can tell'. A2 conversational stretch.

exercises.prompt_en: Fill the blank in this line:

exercises.excerpt: If I was a sculptor, ha
exercises.teacher_note: Auto-generated from synced lyrics — add the model translation when reviewing.

exercises.prompt_en: Write the full form of each contraction:
exercises.prompt_pt: Escreva a forma completa de cada contração:

exercises.prompt_en: Imagine you are making a short film with "Your Song" as the soundtrack. What scene would you show while the chorus plays?
exercises.prompt_pt: Imagine que você vai fazer um curta com "Your Song" como trilha sonora. Que cena você mostraria no momento do refrão?

exercises.prompt_en: Match each English word or phrase to its Portuguese meaning.
exercises.prompt_pt: Associe cada palavra ou expressão em inglês ao significado em português.

exercises.prompt_en: Put the words in the right order:
exercises.prompt_pt: Coloque as palavras na ordem certa:

exercises.prompt_en: How many times do you hear the word "hope" in the whole song?
exercises.prompt_pt: Quantas vezes você ouve a palavra "hope" na música inteira?

exercises.prompt_en: Put these three lines in the order they appear in the song:
exercises.prompt_pt: Coloque estes três versos na ordem em que aparecem na música:

excerpts.text: Yours are the sweetest eyes I've ever seen

teaching_notes_md: **Pedagogy:** Verses show the narrator doubting his ability to write — great entry into hedging language ('kind of funny', 'I don't have much money').

lines: That I put down in the words
How wonderful life is while you're in the world
I'm not one of those who can easily hide
I don't have much money, but boy if I did
